title: Sandboxing & Process Isolation
topic: Sandboxing and process isolation for running untrusted code (like AI-agent workloads): Linux namespaces, cgroups, seccomp-bpf, capabilities, chroot/pivot_root, containers vs microVMs (gVisor, Firecracker), WebAssembly sandboxes, and capability-based security models.
summary: A warden's tour of a maximum-security facility where the inmates are processes: namespaces are the walls, cgroups the rations, capabilities the confiscated keys, and seccomp the visiting rules. Climbs the isolation stack primitive by primitive, tells the real historical container escapes (Dirty COW, runc CVE-2019-5736, Leaky Vessels, the NVIDIA toolkit TOCTOU) as instructive capers, then reaches the stronger cells — Firecracker/gVisor microVMs and WebAssembly's capability-based sandbox — closing with a decision guide for layering them by threat model. Every metaphor maps onto the real mechanism, and the actual syscall, flag, or CVE is always named.

concepts.title: Intake: Who Are You Locking Up?
Namespaces: The Walls of the Facility
Cgroups: Rationing Food, Water, and Air
chroot & pivot_root: Rebuilding the Prisoner's World
The Cell Assembled: What a Container Really Is
Notable Escapes: A History of Breakouts
Defense in Depth: Choosing Your Layers

images.prompt: A wide technical blueprint-style illustration on a deep navy ground with a faint cyan grid: an architectural plan-view of a maximum-security prison, but each cell holds a small glowing terminal/process glyph (a >_ prompt, no readable body text) instead of a figure. Concentric containment rings radiate outward — an inner ring of cell walls, a middle ring labeled with schematic gate/valve symbols, an outer hardened perimeter wall — like layers of defense in depth. Clean vector line-art, engineering-schematic aesthetic, cyan (#46b1e0) and soft teal (#7fd1c8) linework on navy (#0c2233), amber (#f0a15a) accent highlight on one perimeter gate, generous whitespace, no baked-in body text. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape; compose for a hero banner with the subject centered and safe margins — it is letterboxed into a 16:9 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.
images.alt: A blueprint schematic of a maximum-security facility whose cells hold process icons, ringed by concentric isolation walls
images.caption: The facility, drawn as a plan: concentric rings of containment around each caged process.

images.prompt: A technical blueprint-style exploded-assembly diagram on a deep navy ground with a faint cyan grid, showing a prison cell being assembled from stacked transparent layers, like an engineering parts-diagram. Each layer is a labeled pane sliding into place around a small glowing terminal glyph (>_) at the center: an outer layer of walls, a layer of gauges/valves (rations), a layer of keys (privilege), a layer of a barred window (syscall filter), and a floor plate. Clean vector line-art, engineering-schematic aesthetic, cyan (#46b1e0) and soft teal (#7fd1c8) linework on navy (#0c2233), amber (#f0a15a) accents on the assembly arrows, generous whitespace, no readable body text baked in. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape; compose for an on-page figure with the subject centered and safe margins — it is letterboxed into a 16:9 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.
images.alt: An exploded schematic of a container assembled from labeled layers: namespaces, cgroups, capabilities, seccomp, pivot_root
images.caption: A container is an assembly, not a kernel object: primitives snapped together into one cell.

images.prompt: A technical blueprint-style comparison diagram on a deep navy ground with a faint cyan grid, showing three side-by-side vertical stacks that compare isolation boundaries. Stack 1 'container': a small terminal glyph (>_) sitting directly on one thick shared floor labeled with a kernel symbol, a red hairline crack in that floor. Stack 2 'gVisor': the glyph sits on a thin intermediate slab (a userspace kernel) that rests on the shared floor. Stack 3 'microVM': the glyph sits on its own separate floor slab, with a distinct hardened hardware boundary layer beneath it, clearly separated from the host floor. Clean vector line-art, engineering-schematic aesthetic, cyan (#46b1e0) and soft teal (#7fd1c8) linework on navy (#0c2233), one amber (#f0a15a) accent and one red (#e0708a) crack, generous whitespace, no readable body text baked in. Target size ~1280x720px, 16:9 landscape; compose for an on-page figure with the subject centered and safe margins — it is letterboxed into a 16:9 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.
images.alt: Three stacked isolation models compared: a container sharing the host kernel, gVisor interposing a userspace kernel, and a microVM with a separate guest kernel and hardware boundary
images.caption: Three floors: a container shares the host kernel; gVisor slips a userspace kernel between; a microVM builds a separate floor with a hardware boundary beneath.
